title: Generated column wrong value with ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE
description:
## Bug Report

Please answer these questions before submitting your issue. Thanks!

### 1. Minimal reproduce step (Required)

```sql
CREATE TABLE t(
	id INT PRIMARY KEY,
	text1 TEXT,
	text2 TEXT,
	text3 TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (CONCAT(text1, text2)) STORED,
	vec TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (REPEAT(text3, 2)) STORED,
	text4 TEXT GENERATED ALWAYS AS (REPEAT(text1, 2)) STORED
);

INSERT INTO t VALUES (1, "foo", "bar", DEFAULT, DEFAULT, DEFAULT);

INSERT INTO t (id, text1, text2) VALUES (1, "foo2", "bar2") ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE text1 = "foo3", text2 = "bar3";

SELECT * FROM t;

```

<!-- a step by step guide for reproducing the bug. -->

### 2. What did you expect to see? (Required)

MySQL:

```
+----+-------+-------+----------+------------------+----------+
| id | text1 | text2 | text3    | vec              | text4    |
+----+-------+-------+----------+------------------+----------+
|  1 | foo3  | bar3  | foo3bar3 | foo3bar3foo3bar3 | foo3foo3 |
+----+-------+-------+----------+------------------+----------+
```

### 3. What did you see instead (Required)

TiDB nightly:

```
+----+-------+-------+----------+--------------+----------+
| id | text1 | text2 | text3    | vec          | text4    |
+----+-------+-------+----------+--------------+----------+
|  1 | foo3  | bar3  | foo3bar3 | foobarfoobar | foo3foo3 |
+----+-------+-------+----------+--------------+----------+
```

### 4. What is your TiDB version? (Required)

<!-- Paste the output of SELECT tidb_version() -->

```
Release Version: v9.0.0-beta.2.pre-76-g9accc3cfa3
Edition: Community
Git Commit Hash: 9accc3cfa3de44130537fcf9767623c436869aa6
Git Branch: HEAD
UTC Build Time: 2025-07-08 05:05:26
GoVersion: go1.23.10
Race Enabled: false
Check Table Before Drop: false
Store: tikv
Kernel Type: Classic
```
bug_features_seed: CREATE TABLE t2 (id Int32 PRIMARY KEY, text1 String, text2 String, text3 String DEFAULT CONCAT(text1, ' ', text2), vec String DEFAULT REPEAT(text3, 2));
INSERT INTO t2 (id, text1, text2) VALUES (1, 'goodbye', 'earth') ON CONFLICT (id) DO UPDATE SET text1 = 'farewell', text2 = 'planet';
CREATE TABLE t3 (id Int32 PRIMARY KEY, num1 Int32, num2 Int32, sum_col Int32 DEFAULT (num1 + num2));
key_bug_features: ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE with generated columns
